definition: Fed, ECB, and BoJ balance sheet operations represent fundamentally different interventions despite the unified label 'normalization.' The Fed does passive runoff in deep liquid markets; the ECB manages sovereign credit allocation across 20 members with fragmentation risk; the BoJ holds >50% of JGBs and IS the market. Drawing QT conclusions across these is a category error.
evidence: Fed: passive Treasury/MBS runoff in deep, liquid markets
ECB: TLTRO direct credit effects, BTP-Bund dynamics, 20 sovereign members
Confirmed in pair_3 debate as most defensible claim (challenger_01 at 9/10)
open_questions: Can BoJ normalization proceed without major yield increases?
Does the divergence create exploitable cross-market dislocations?
